QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_61

question.text: Vikram, a private sector employee, has a basic salary of Rs.8 lakh and DA of Rs.2 lakh. His employer contributes 12% to NPS. What is the maximum deductible under 80CCD(2)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For private sector employees, 80CCD(2) allows employer contribution deduction up to 10% of Basic + DA. Basic + DA = Rs.10 lakh. 10% = Rs.1 lakh. Even though employer contributes 12%, only 10% is deductible. **The Trap:** The employer contributes 12% (Rs.1.2 lakh) but deduction is capped at 10% (Rs.1 lakh). The excess Rs.20,000 is taxable as salary. **Cross-Exam Context:** 14% limit is only for Central Government. Private sector cap is 10% of Basic + DA under 80CCD(2). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Advise private sector employers to contribute exactly 10% to NPS to maximize tax efficiency. Contributions above 10% create a tax liability.
